Title: A TaSnRK1α Modulates TaPAP6L-Mediated Wheat Cold Tolerance through Regulating Endogenous Jasmonic Acid

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastid-lipid-associated protein 6, chloroplastic-like; PAP/FBN fibrillin family
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fielder; Kronos; Chinese Spring; Keyu 368; Xinong 18
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively increases JA content and enhances wheat cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation; pull-down; BiFC; EMS mutants; overexpression; transcriptome sequencing; JA quantification; in vivo and in vitro phosphorylation assays; haplotype association
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaPAP6L-2B interacts with TaSnRK1α-1A and is phosphorylated by it, which increases TaPAP6L-2B protein accumulation. 2. Overexpression elevates JA content and enhances cold tolerance, while EMS mutants reduce JA and cold tolerance. 3. TaPAP6L-2B likely raises JA by promoting α-linolenic acid precursor synthesis and repressing JAR1-mediated JA conversion to JA-Ile.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter for wheat overexpression; 35S promoter in tobacco transient assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding cold-tolerant wheat, especially favorable phospho-enhanced haplotypes such as TaPAP6L-2B Hap6 / R30S G38S, potentially stacked with TaSnRK1α
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among homoeologs TaPAP6L-2A, -2B, and -2D, TaPAP6L-2B showed the highest phosphorylation by TaSnRK1α-1A; favorable haplotype changes R30S and G38S enhanced phosphorylation and cold tolerance

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables TaSnRK1α-1A kinase activity in vitro, thereby supporting phosphorylation of TaPAP6L-2B
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGRIK1 alone did not phosphorylate TaPAP6L-2B. 2. Addition of TaGRIK1 enabled TaSnRK1α-1A to phosphorylate TaPAP6L-2B in vitro. 3. TaGRIK1 functions as an activator of TaSnRK1α-1A kinase activity in the demonstrated pathway.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supportive mechanistic component, but direct breeding value for JA/cold phenotype was not established in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly phenotyped in planta for JA or cold tolerance in this study

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK1 alpha catalytic subunit; sucrose non-fermenting-1-related protein kinase alpha subunit
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fielder; Kronos; Zhengmai 9023; Shanghai3/Catbird; Chinese Spring
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by cold stress; identified from cold-tolerant versus cold-sensitive pools after 24 h and 48 h cold treatment
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; cytoplasm; enriched to chloroplast under cold stress with TaDJ-1 assistance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress; TaGRIK1 activates TaSnRK1α kinase activity in vitro; TaDJ-1 facilitates chloroplast targeting
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein phosphorylation and positive regulation of target protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonic acid accumulation via control of α-linolenic acid-associated precursor supply and reduced JA degradation through TaPAP6L
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Phosphorylates TaPAP6L-2B and increases its protein abundance; indirectly associated with repression of JAR1 and promotion of α-linolenic acid synthesis genes through TaPAP6L
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with TaDJ-1 and TaPAP6L-2B in a chloroplast-associated interaction module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively increases endogenous jasmonic acid and enhances wheat cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream controller of JA accumulation under cold stress through phosphorylation of TaPAP6L-2B
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; BSR-seq; VIGS; EMS mutants; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation; pull-down; BiFC; in vivo and in vitro phosphorylation assays; phytohormone quantification
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaSnRK1α-1A was identified as the major cold-tolerance candidate by GWAS, BSR-seq, and VIGS. 2. Overexpression increased, while EMS mutation or CRISPR editing decreased, wheat cold tolerance and JA content. 3. TaSnRK1α-1A interacts with and phosphorylates TaPAP6L-2B, promoting TaPAP6L protein accumulation and thereby supporting JA-mediated cold tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter for wheat overexpression; 35S promoter in tobacco transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulatory gene for breeding or engineering wheat cold tolerance by increasing endogenous JA, potentially stacked with TaPAP6L favorable haplotypes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hexaploid and tetraploid validation systems were both used; kinase activity required TaGRIK1 in vitro

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Supports JA-related cold tolerance pathway by enabling chloroplast localization of TaSnRK1α-1A and its interaction with TaPAP6L-2B
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Facilitates TaSnRK1α-1A movement to chloroplast and promotes TaSnRK1α-1A/TaPAP6L-2B interaction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly promotes JA-linked cold tolerance pathway by enabling TaSnRK1α-1A chloroplast interaction with TaPAP6L-2B
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-supporting facilitator rather than direct determinant; required for efficient TaSnRK1α-1A chloroplast association
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation; subcellular localization; VIGS; BiFC; chloroplast protein immunoblot
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaDJ-1-7B interacts with both TaSnRK1α-1A and TaPAP6L-2B. 2. Silencing TaDJ-1 weakens TaSnRK1α-1A/TaPAP6L-2B interaction and prevents TaSnRK1α-1A detection in chloroplasts. 3. TaDJ-1 likely acts as a molecular chaperone bridging the regulatory module that promotes JA-mediated cold tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive target for optimizing TaSnRK1α/TaPAP6L signaling, though direct breeding value was not fully established
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main evidence supports a bridging/chaperone role; direct effect on JA content was not measured in this study
